steps.title: Identify Brown's finding and the mechanism behind it
steps.content:
The passage states that high moonlight inhibits Arizona pocket mouse activity because the benefit (no meaningful increase in foraging success) does not offset the cost (higher chance of being spotted by predatory owls or hawks). The key phrase is "greater lunar intensity may not enable the mice to increase foraging success enough to offset the higher chance of detection."

Now eliminate each choice:
  • "Arizona pocket mice tend to be more active during periods of high lunar intensity than at other times because such conditions allow them to increase their foraging success without making them easier to detect.": the text says the opposite — activity is inhibited, not increased. ✗
  • "During periods of high lunar intensity, Arizona pocket mice reduce their activity because it is easier for predators to detect the mice in relatively bright conditions than it is for predators to detect three-striped night monkeys in such conditions.": introduces a species comparison the text never makes — monkeys are mentioned for having the opposite response, not for being harder to detect. ✗
  • "Arizona pocket mice reduce their activity during periods of high lunar intensity because predators can more easily spot the mice in brighter conditions and such conditions do not benefit the mice enough to justify that risk.": matches the two-part mechanism exactly: increased detection risk + insufficient foraging benefit. ✓
  • "During periods of high lunar intensity three-striped night monkeys show a different behavioral response than Arizona pocket mice and many other nocturnal mammals do because the risks to three-striped night monkeys under such conditions are greater.": the text says monkeys rely heavily on visual foraging, giving them a different benefit-risk balance — not that their risks are greater. ✗

steps.title: Confirm and close
steps.content:
Arizona pocket mice reduce their activity during periods of high lunar intensity because predators can more easily spot the mice in brighter conditions and such conditions do not benefit the mice enough to justify that risk. captures both parts of Brown's finding: brighter moonlight raises detection risk for the mice, and that risk is not offset by any meaningful foraging advantage.

That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
